stem: A agriculture study measures the yield per hectare (tonnes) for a sample of 15 farms. The sample mean is 343.7 tonnes with a sample standard deviation of 71.4 tonnes. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 334.3 tonnes and standard deviation of 52.1 tonnes. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 343.7, 95% CI = [304.1, 383.2]. Without outliers: mean = 334.3, 95% CI = [305.4, 363.2]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [304.1, 383.2]. Point estimate: 339.0 tonnes.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [304.1, 383.2].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
